summary: The reproduction logs in as a low-privilege custom user, calls POST /api/core/restart-master, and confirms that the same session cookie is invalid after the Ajenti master restart fires.
expected_security_impact: A low-privilege authenticated user should be able to trigger a control-plane restart of Ajenti and cause the previously valid session cookie to fail on a protected endpoint after the restart.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh started Ajenti with a low-privilege custom user, obtained a valid session, received HTTP 200 from POST /api/core/restart-master, observed the restart log message, and then saw the same cookie receive HTTP 401 from /api/core/sidebar after the restart.
verification_method: The verifier checks three signals together: successful low-privilege login, HTTP 200 from the restart route, and HTTP 401 from a protected endpoint when reusing the same cookie after a short delay. It also greps the Ajenti log for the restart message to distinguish a real restart from a generic auth failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: The restart request returned HTTP 200 for the low-privilege session.
The same cookie later returned HTTP 401 on /api/core/sidebar.
Ajenti logged the restart signal "Will restart the process now".

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
